subject: Gaming
title: Sonic vs Mario: The Mascot War
description: A quick, practical guide to Sonic vs Mario: The Mascot War.

hook.text: Sega created Sonic the Hedgehog with one goal: destroy Mario. The 1990s mascot war had begun.

buildup.text: Sonic was designed as everything Mario wasn't—fast, cool, and marketed directly to teenagers.

discovery.text: The Genesis outsold the SNES for two holiday seasons. 'Genesis does what Nintendon't' was devastating.

twist.text: Nintendo fought back with Super Mario World and deeper RPGs. The war split an entire generation of kids.

climax.text: The 16-bit war was gaming's first culture war—playground arguments defined by which console you owned.

punchline.text: Hedgehog vs plumber. Every kid had to pick a side.

quiz.question: Why did Sega create Sonic the Hedgehog?
options: To directly compete with Mario as a cooler, faster mascot
As a spin-off of an existing Sega character
